reasoning: The flagged vulnerability in sign_update.rb is macOS-specific and not executed during Arch Linux build/install. PKGBUILD and build scripts show no malicious behavior relevant to Arch.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged an unused macOS script as critical risk; DeepSeek focused on Arch-relevant files but missed OSX directory review.
coverage_issues: DeepSeek skipped OSX directory entirely; Qwen under-prioritized build scripts like countries-build.pl that perform network operations
Neither reviewed all files with network/filesystem access during build (e.g., mapscroller.pl)
false_positives: sign_update.rb: Command injection risk exists but is irrelevant as script isn't invoked during makepkg (macOS-only tooling)
learnings: File selection: Prioritize OS-specific relevance (e.g., skip macOS scripts for Arch audits) and verify build process involvement
Assessment quality: Improve context awareness - distinguish between actual build risks vs. unused development tool vulnerabilities
Failure modes: Reduce over-alerting on non-build files; Implement build-process tracing to verify script execution paths
